subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 2 to 5 players.
- **Scaling** — VP chip pool scales by player count (12 per player).
- **Solo mode** — Not included in the base game.

#### Dice
- **White dice (Home)** — 5 per player starting dice.
- **Colored dice** — Additional dice gained from tiles: Red (military), Blue (novelty goods), Brown (rare elements), Green (genes), Yellow (alien technology), Purple (developments).
- **Die faces** — Each die color has a different distribution of phase symbols: Explore, Develop, Settle, Produce, Ship, and Wild ($).

#### Tiles
- **Faction tiles** — 9 double-sided faction tiles (starting tableau).
- **Game tiles** — 55 development and world tiles in a bag.
- **Tile types** — Developments (diamond icon) and worlds (circle icon), each with VP values and abilities.

#### Other Components
- **VP chips** — 1-point and 5-point chips (12 per player in the pool).
- **Credit tokens** — $1 tokens for purchasing.
- **Player screens** — Cup/screen for each player to roll and assign dice secretly.
- **Phase tiles** — 5 phase strip tiles for each player.
- **Citizenry cup** — Area for unused dice available for recruitment.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Table Setup
1. **VP chip pool** — Place 12 VP chips per player in the center.
2. **Tile bag** — Place all 55 game tiles in the draw bag.
3. **Dice supply** — Sort colored dice by type in a central supply.
4. **Credit supply** — Place credit tokens in a central pool.
5. **Phase reference** — Place the round summary tile where all can see.

--- 2 Players ---
- **VP pool** — 24 VP chips.

--- 3 Players ---
- **VP pool** — 36 VP chips.

--- 4 Players ---
- **VP pool** — 48 VP chips.

--- 5 Players ---
- **VP pool** — 60 VP chips.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Faction tile** — Each player receives 1 random faction tile and places it face up.
- **Starting dice** — Take the starting dice shown on your faction tile (typically 3 white dice plus extras based on faction).
- **Starting tiles** — Draw tiles from the bag as indicated by your faction.
- **Screen** — Take a player screen to hide dice assignments.
- **Phase strip** — Place your phase strip behind your screen.
- **Citizenry** — Place any remaining starting dice in your Citizenry (available for recruitment).
- **Credits** — Start with 1 credit (unless faction says otherwise).

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Beginning Play
- **Simultaneous play** — All players act simultaneously each round.
- **No first player** — There is no turn order advantage since play is simultaneous.
- **Initial roll** — All players roll their dice behind their screens to begin the first round.
- **Assign and select** — Assign dice to phases and select one die for your chosen phase.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Roll** — Each player rolls all their active dice behind their screen.
2. **Assign** — Secretly assign dice to phases on your phase strip based on what they rolled.
3. **Select phase** — Choose 1 die and place it on the phase you want to activate (this die dictates your chosen phase).
4. **Reveal** — All players lift screens simultaneously; only chosen phases are active this round.
5. **Resolve phases** — Execute active phases in order: Explore, Develop, Settle, Produce, Ship.
6. **Recall** — Return used dice to your cup; manage Citizenry and credits.
7. **Reassign** — Dice assigned to inactive phases return to your cup for next round.

#### Key Rule
- **Only chosen phases happen** — If no player chose a phase, it is skipped entirely.
- **Reassignment** — Dice placed on unchosen phases are not lost; they return to your cup.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Explore Phase
- **Stock** — Draw tiles from the bag and credits based on dice assigned.
- **Each die** — Each Explore die lets you draw 1 tile from the bag and gain $1.
- **Keep limit** — Keep tiles up to the number of Explore dice used, discard the rest.

#### Develop Phase
- **Build developments** — Assign Develop dice to developments under construction.
- **Cost** — Each development has a die cost (number of dice needed to complete it).
- **Completion** — When enough dice are placed, the development enters your tableau.

#### Settle Phase
- **Settle worlds** — Assign Settle dice to worlds under construction.
- **Non-military** — Pay the world's die cost with Settle dice.
- **Military** — Military worlds require military dice (red) equal to their defense value.

#### Produce Phase
- **Place goods** — Each Produce die placed on an empty production world creates a good (a die sits on the world as the good).
- **One good per world** — Each production world can hold only 1 good.

#### Ship Phase
- **Trade** — Ship 1 good for credits and VP (trade value depends on good type).
- **Consume** — Use tableau consume powers to convert goods to VP chips.
- **Each Ship die** — Enables shipping one good.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Dice Management
- **Citizenry** — Dice not in your cup sit in your Citizenry; recruit them by paying credits.
- **Recruitment** — Pay $1 per die to move dice from Citizenry to your cup for next round's roll.
- **Gaining dice** — Completing tiles that grant dice adds them to your Citizenry.
- **Wild faces ($)** — Dice showing the Wild ($) face can be assigned to any phase.

#### Tile Construction
- **Under construction** — Tiles from Explore go under construction; they are not in your tableau yet.
- **Incremental building** — You can add dice to a tile across multiple rounds.
- **Completion** — Once a tile has enough dice, it enters your tableau and its powers activate.

#### Dice Colors and Strategy
- **White (Home)** — Balanced face distribution.
- **Red (Military)** — Heavy on Settle faces; needed for military worlds.
- **Blue/Brown/Green/Yellow** — Biased toward Produce and Ship faces.
- **Purple** — Biased toward Develop faces.

#### Reassignment
- **Dictate ability** — Some tile powers let you reassign dice from one phase to another.
- **Flexibility** — Reassignment is a powerful ability that lets you use dice more efficiently.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Triggers (either one)
- **Tableau size** — Any player completes their 12th tile in their tableau.
- **VP pool empty** — The VP chip pool runs out during shipping.

#### Final Round
- **Complete the round** — Finish the current round through all active phases.

#### Scoring
- **Tile VP** — Sum VP printed on all tiles in your tableau.
- **Bonus VP** — Some tiles grant conditional endgame VP based on tableau composition.
- **Shipping VP** — Add all VP chips accumulated during the game.
- **Total** — Highest total wins.

#### Tiebreaker
- **Most dice plus credits** — Player with the most total dice (cup + Citizenry) plus credits wins ties.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Faction synergy** — Your starting faction dictates your early strategy; lean into its strengths.
- **Build production early** — Getting production worlds online creates a reliable Produce/Ship VP engine.
- **Recruit dice** — Spending credits to recruit dice from Citizenry increases your output each round.
- **Explore for options** — Early Explore phases build a pipeline of tiles to develop and settle.
- **Watch die colors** — Dice colors bias toward certain phases; plan your engine around the dice you acquire.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring Citizenry** — Dice stuck in Citizenry do nothing; recruit them actively.
- **Over-exploring** — Drawing too many tiles without building them wastes rounds.
- **Neglecting Ship phase** — Goods sitting on worlds are worth nothing until shipped for VP.
- **Forgetting reassignment** — Dice on unchosen phases return to your cup; plan around this.
- **Poor die color management** — Acquiring dice that do not match your strategy leads to wasted rolls.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Produce/Ship vs Develop/Settle
- **Produce/Ship engine** — Generates steady VP chips through goods conversion.
- **Develop/Settle rush** — Builds tableau quickly to end the game with high tile VP.

#### Decision 2: Which Phase to Select
- **Self-benefit** — Choose the phase that helps you most.
- **Opponent reading** — Predict what others will choose to get free phase access.

#### Decision 3: Dice Recruitment vs Saving Credits
- **Recruit now** — More dice means more options and output next round.
- **Save credits** — Credits are also needed for trading and some tile abilities.
